refinement_sections.content: We need to create a sorting algorithm for customer records. These records might include names, emails, and phone numbers. The sorting needs to be efficient, which means it should work quickly. Also, we will have many searches and updates happening at the same time.

1. The algorithm should sort customer records by name, email, and phone number. 2. It should be efficient, meaning it should take less time to sort. 3. We need to think about how much memory it uses; it should not use too much. 4. The system must handle many updates at once without crashing.
1. We should consider the size of the dataset. 2. The algorithm needs to be able to sort and search quickly. 3. We should keep in mind that we need to save memory.
1. Look into existing sorting algorithms like quicksort or mergesort. 2. Consider if we need to use any data structures that help with searching, like hash tables or trees.
1. How fast the sorting is done (time complexity). 2. How much memory the algorithm uses (space complexity). 3. How well it handles many updates at the same time.
